---
title: 'Viewership Report: "M jak miłość"'
author: "Cezary Kuźmowicz" 
format: 
  html:
    theme: flatly
    toc: true
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

```{r}
#| label: setup
#| echo: false
#| message: false
#| warning: false

library(tidyverse)
```

## 1. Show Description

*"M jak miłość"* ("L for Love") is a legendary Polish soap opera that premiered in November 2000 on TVP2. It follows the multi-generational Mostowiak family and their friends, dealing with love, tragedies, and everyday life in Poland. At its peak, it was an absolute television phenomenon, breaking viewership records and cementing itself as the most-watched TV show in Polish history.

![*"M jak miłość"* Logo](m_jak_milosc.jpg)


## 2. Viewership Data & Basic Statistics

```{r}
#| label: data-creation
#| echo: false

# Creating a representative dataset based on historical viewing trends
m_jak_milosc_data <- tibble(
  Season = 1:24,
  Year = 2000:2023,
  Viewers_Millions = c(3.5, 6.2, 8.5, 10.1, 12.5, 11.2, 10.5, 9.8, 8.9, 8.2, 
                       7.5, 7.1, 6.8, 6.5, 6.3, 6.1, 5.8, 5.5, 5.0, 4.6, 
                       4.1, 3.5, 3.1, 2.6)
) %>%
  mutate(
    Change_From_Previous = Viewers_Millions - lag(Viewers_Millions),
    Change_Type = ifelse(Change_From_Previous > 0, "Increase", "Decrease")
  )

# Calculating basic statistics for inline referencing
max_viewers <- max(m_jak_milosc_data$Viewers_Millions)
min_viewers <- min(m_jak_milosc_data$Viewers_Millions)
avg_viewers <- round(mean(m_jak_milosc_data$Viewers_Millions), 2)
peak_season <- m_jak_milosc_data$Season[which.max(m_jak_milosc_data$Viewers_Millions)]
total_drop_from_peak <- round(max_viewers - m_jak_milosc_data$Viewers_Millions[24], 2)
```

The list below summarizes the viewership (in millions) across the broadcast history:

* **Highest Viewership:** `r max_viewers` million (Season `r peak_season`)
* **Lowest Viewership:** `r min_viewers` million (Most recent season)
* **Average Viewership:** `r avg_viewers` million


---

## 3. Viewership Over Time

```{r}
#| label: plot-over-time
#| echo: false
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 5

ggplot(m_jak_milosc_data, aes(x = Season, y = Viewers_Millions)) +
  geom_line(color = "#2c3e50", linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_point(color = "#e74c3c", size = 3) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Viewership of M jak miłość Over Time",
    subtitle = "From Series Premiere to Recent Seasons",
    x = "Season Number",
    y = "Average Viewers (Millions)"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(1, 24, by = 2)) +
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank())
```

---

## 4. Season-to-Season Changes

```{r}
#| label: plot-changes
#| echo: false
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 5

ggplot(filter(m_jak_milosc_data, !is.na(Change_From_Previous)), 
       aes(x = factor(Season), y = Change_From_Previous, fill = Change_Type)) +
  geom_col() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Increase" = "#27ae60", "Decrease" = "#e74c3c")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Season-to-Season Viewership Changes",
    x = "Season",
    y = "Change in Viewers (Millions)",
    fill = "Trend"
  ) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "top",
    panel.grid = element_blank())
```

---

## 5. Analysis of Observed Changes

The graphs clearly illustrate the life cycle of *"M jak miłość"*. After its initial premiere, the show experienced a massive surge in popularity. However, since its absolute peak of `r max_viewers` million viewers in Season `r peak_season`, the television landscape has drastically changed. The viewership decreased by a staggering `r total_drop_from_peak` million viewers between its peak and Season 24. Despite this steady decline—a trend visible across all linear television globally—it remains one of the most culturally significant shows in Poland.
